name: Binance
one_liner: World's largest cryptocurrency exchange offering spot, futures, margin, options, and wallet trading via a broad public REST/WebSocket/FIX API.
access_notes: Anyone can create working API credentials on the Spot Testnet (testnet.binance.vision) by logging in with a GitHub account, with no payment or approval. Production credentials require a verified Binance account, but the free testnet gives immediately-usable keys for building/testing.
api_notes: Extensive API surface: Spot, USDⓈ-M and COIN-M Futures, Options, Margin, Wallet, and Convert. Offered via REST, WebSocket API, WebSocket Streams, and FIX API. Covers market data (order books, klines, tickers, trades) plus full order lifecycle (place/cancel/amend/query, OCO order lists) and account/user-data operations — well over 100 endpoints.
mcp_notes: No vendor-shipped MCP server found. Multiple community/unofficial MCP servers exist (e.g. TermiX-official/binance-mcp, ethancod1ng/binance-mcp-server, snjyor/binance-mcp) wrapping the Binance REST API for LLM trading/market-data access.
main_blocker: None significant for building: public documented API with self-serve testnet keys. Request signing (HMAC/RSA/Ed25519) via X-MBX-APIKEY header adds mild complexity, and production trading requires a verified account.

evidence.claim: Spot Testnet API keys can be created by logging in with a GitHub account; key goes in X-MBX-APIKEY header; supports HMAC, RSA, and Ed25519 keys; base endpoint https://testnet.binance.vision/api.
API supports REST, WebSocket API, WebSocket Streams, FIX API, and SBE; covers Spot, Futures, Options, Margin, Wallet, Convert with market-data and full order-management endpoints.
Official Binance Spot API documentation and streams repository maintained by Binance.

open_questions: Whether any production-account restrictions (regional/KYC) meaningfully limit agent-driven live trading; no official vendor MCP as of research date.
